label: Rare Fish Crowding Near Lake Huron Nuclear Plant

comments.text: It's always surreal to see the size of mammoth fish that people can pull out of Lake Ontario from the downtown shoreline with the CN Tower in the background

Rare footage reveals 'fish city' near an #Ontario nuclear plant on Lake Huron
Ahead of an Earth Day livestream, CBC News got a rare look at fish crowding near a nuclear plant www.cbc.ca/news/science...
In the warm waters flowing from an Ontario nuclear plant, fish are gathering by the thousands in Lake Huron.

We have wind farms in Ontario, near some of the Great Lakes. They do create a low noise that might not be good to have near homes, but most of them are sited in fields. And their lazy rotation is a hell of a lot more soothing to watch than a nuclear plant would be.

I toured a big data center near Toronto waterfront once. Cooling down by drawing deep water from Lake Ontario, 4-7 oC year around, even during hottest summer day. Similar to Pickering Nuclear plant. There's an Internet gateway near base of CN Tower, where numerous fibre optics cables converge.

In the warm waters emanating from Ontario's largest nuclear plant, fish are gathering by the thousands. Ahead of an Earth Day livestream, CBC News got a rare look at what some call a 'fish city,' a striking scene scientists say may signal a changing Great Lakes ecosystem.
www.cbc.ca/news/science...
